heroHeadline: 11th Circuit Allows 'Alligator Alcatraz' Everglades Detention to Stay Open; IL Court Protects ICE-Monitoring Apps; FY2026 Deaths Hit 29
heroSubtitle: The 11th Circuit Court of Appeals on April 21 vacated an injunction blocking ICE's controversial 'Alligator Alcatraz' detention facility near the Florida Everglades, allowing it to remain open while environmental litigation continues; a federal judge in Illinois separately ruled on April 18 that the Trump administration violated the First Amendment by pressuring Apple and Meta to remove ICE-monitoring apps used by immigrant communities; ICE custody deaths in FY2026 stand at 29 (through April 18) — a 22-year record surpassing the 28 deaths in FY2004; acting ICE Director Todd Lyons will depart May 31 with no successor named; ICE FY2025 total removals: 442,637
footerNote: Data sourced from DHS, ICE official records, ACLU, HRW, DHS OIG, TRAC Reports, Detention Watch Network, court records, and Congressional records
